name: Ebastine
description: Ebastine is under investigation for the treatment of Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS). Ebastine has been investigated for the treatment of Urticaria.

interactions.name: Idelalisib
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Ebastine can be increased when it is combined with Idelalisib.

interactions.name: Ribociclib
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Ebastine can be increased when it is combined with Ribociclib.

interactions.name: Brigatinib
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Ebastine can be decreased when it is combined with Brigatinib.

interactions.name: Benzylpenicilloyl Polylysine
interactions.description: Ebastine may decrease effectiveness of Benzylpenicilloyl Polylysine as a diagnostic agent.

interactions.name: Hyaluronidase
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Hyaluronidase can be decreased when used in combination with Ebastine.

interactions.name: Betahistine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Betahistine can be decreased when used in combination with Ebastine.

interactions.name: Pseudoephedrine
interactions.description: Pseudoephedrine may decrease the sedative activities of Ebastine.

interactions.name: Diethylpropion
interactions.description: Diethylpropion may decrease the sedative activities of Ebastine.

interactions.name: Lisdexamfetamine
interactions.description: Lisdexamfetamine may decrease the sedative activities of Ebastine.

interactions.name: Dextroamphetamine
interactions.description: Dextroamphetamine may decrease the sedative activities of Ebastine.

interactions.name: Amphetamine
interactions.description: Amphetamine may decrease the sedative activities of Ebastine.

interactions.name: Phentermine
interactions.description: Phentermine may decrease the sedative activities of Ebastine.

interactions.name: Benzphetamine

interactions.name: Mephentermine
interactions.description: Mephentermine may decrease the sedative activities of Ebastine.

interactions.name: Midomafetamine
interactions.description: Midomafetamine may decrease the sedative activities of Ebastine.

interactions.name: 2,5-Dimethoxy-4-ethylamphetamine
interactions.description: 2,5-Dimethoxy-4-ethylamphetamine may decrease the sedative activities of Ebastine.

interactions.name: 4-Bromo-2,5-dimethoxyamphetamine
interactions.description: 4-Bromo-2,5-dimethoxyamphetamine may decrease the sedative activities of Ebastine.

interactions.name: 3,4-Methylenedioxyamphetamine
interactions.description: 3,4-Methylenedioxyamphetamine may decrease the sedative activities of Ebastine.

interactions.name: Chlorphentermine
